reviews.review_title: Good game! But needs an update
reviews.review_description:
The mushroom items are very op (in the sense that they doesn’t break forever when hit), and also the mushroom items sometimes get de-synced to where I hit them and the squirrels can still bounce on them. 

We probably need more maps (and maybe more tree types?) so the game doesn’t get repetitive.

Besides that though it’s a very fun game and I recommend it as a play with family in the living room game. please make more asymmetrical vr-mobile games please ok bye :)

reviews.review_title: Game is fun but a bit overpriced
reviews.review_description:
Game was released in 2019 and standard price is 200dkk ? .. I'm happy i had 50dkk credits and the game is half price with horizon+ 

The game is very fun in a party or together with a group of kids. But i could imagine that it gets repetitive with the amount of maps and squirrels. 

The tree also only have the same weapons in every map? 

Definitely recommend it regardless !

This game is honestly just fun, alone or playing with friends.
I recamend getting this game
Have a great rest of your day guys!!!
The only game for VR + 8 smartphones with crossplay (android + iOS + headset). Apps on smartphones are free. Gameplay is easy. Lot of fun  for my friends and family 7-45 yo. Awesome!
We love this game. The gameplay quality and connection fantastic. Amazing party game. We need more maps and characters!!! Please go buy Taklings House Party and bring it to Quest 2. <——— do it!

I really love the game. There is a bug which would be nice to fix. If the caracter re-spawns sometimes they fall over the ground, intantly knocking out the player. Please fix that. But again, great game and fun to play with the kids.
Allows you to pay for it on a Meta Quest however only installs v17. Installing on phone automatically installs v19 which is incompatible so does not work.

developer_response.body: We are sorry to hear you experienced this. Unfortunately the store page for Quest1 headsets is not within our control. Please contact Meta to get a refund.

reviews.review_description: The game is great, but there is a bug where the acorn becomes unable to be grabbed and it ruins the game. If it didn't have this bug it would be a fun party game. It is very unbalanced but fun.

reviews.review_title: V1.17 on Quest and v1.18 on iphone
reviews.review_description: Please help. I can't install 1.18 on my quest and it won't sync with the phone. I deleted and re-installed on quest, but still received 1.17. I have the first Meta Quest.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing Acron. Meta has discontinued support for Meta Quest 1 which unfortunately makes it impossible for us to maintain the game on that device. Apologies for the inconvenience.

reviews.review_description: Great family game! Please make more!

reviews.review_title: Love the game so here’s suggestion’s
reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love this game, it’s a blast, especially when played with friends and family. That said, its fun factor heavily depends on having others available, and it can start feeling repetitive after a few rounds. With a few thoughtful updates, it could be much more versatile and engaging.

Suggested Improvements:

1. Single-player
	•Solo mode with bots: Many players suggest adding the ability to play by yourself just against bots. This would easily eliminate the issue of not having people to play with.
	•Story mode: I think it would be great if there were an addition of a story mode where waves of squirrels attack you and progressively get harder. This would also add some variation to single-player.

2. Online Matchmaking / Public Rooms
	•Adding an online lobby or matchmaking feature would help users connect with others globally, not just locally.

3. Training / Stats / UI Enhancements
	•Training area: A dedicated practice zone with targets to warm up and improve skills would be valuable.
	•End-of-match stats: Tracking throws, saves, longest distances, accuracy, and most durable squirrels would boost replay value ￼.

4. Balanced Game Modes & Power-ups
	•New modes:
	•Capture Zones: Squirrels capture points on the map  
	•Attack the Tree: Squirrels throw items at the tree to bring it down  ￼
	•VIS (Very Important Squirrel): Escort missions featuring checkpoints, with team strategy  ￼
	•Power-up pickups: Place squirrel power-ups like speed bursts or shields throughout maps to encourage dynamic gameplay.
      •Balancing aids:
	•Scale tree abilities based on squirrel count.
	•Reduce tree’s sap cooldown, puddle duration, and large boulder linger time.

5. Camera & Controls
	•Mobile controls: Improve joystick and camera responsiveness, the current fixed-back view is frustrating when retreating.
	•Phone UX: Fix screen-timeouts and stability issues on Android devices.

6. More Content & Replayability
	•Larger variety of maps: More terrains, new level layouts, and dynamic obstacles would refresh the experience.
	•New squirrel characters & customization:
	•Introduce new squirrel types (e.g. an invisible “Camo” squirrel, female variants)￼.
	•Offer cosmetic and gameplay unlocks over time.

7. Spectator & Shared Experience
	•Spectator mode / Multi-tree: Add a spectator viewpoint or allow two trees, enhancing strategy and engagement, especially for larger groups￼.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your very good suggestions

reviews.review_description: Fantastic game for large groups. Absolutely love!
Very fun when you play with your family
I cannot get v 18 on my oculus. It’s says there is no updates and it won’t allow the room to form. Pls help

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for playing Acron! What Quest device are you playing on? 
I'm sorry to tell you Quest 1 is no longer a supported device as of Metas discontinuation of it in May 2025. Sorry for the inconvenience.
If you are playing on a newer device - please check that your OS is updated and restart the headset.

reviews.review_description: This is an excellent game, lots of fun and you only need one VR headset. I wish there were more games like this. The mobile app is free so you only need to purchase the all for the VR headset. I wholeheartedly recommend this game!

If only there could be other games like this one! I feel so lucky and grateful to be able to play such a party game with my friends who don't own a VR headset. They all love it and appreciate it more than any other VR game out there!

This game was a hit at our little get together. Was fun for adults. Later I let my 7year old and 4 year old play and they were able to play each other after some help.
Highly recommend this game

You can play together 
you have to protect the acorn
if the Squirrels gets all of the acorn The squirrel win
I'm introvert.. Pls make some room for single player mode or just let me play with random players..
I love this game, but don't know anyone else with a headset and the game. The tutorial is so fun, I wish I could play more, or just connect with a stranger to play.
Ultimate Family Party Game <-------that sums it up! We have played this as a family(ages 10, 17, 42, and 58) and we all love it!! Easy to learn and fast paced.
But why is it money now?

Got this game to try and entertain my nieces/nephews, except the adults ended up having just as much if not more fun! Perfect for small parties. Played for almost 3 hours all talking turns as the tree. Acron will be a regular in this house!
It's a great party game. One player (with VR) is the tree. The other players use their phones. Nine people can play at a time.

This game flew way under my radar,I would’ve never even considered giving it a shot. Luckily,it is currently free to play with the Quest+ subscription. I seen it on there and started to skip past it but I’m always looking for games that my 7 & 8 year old nieces might enjoy. When I read the game description,I knew I had to give it a try. My 8 year old niece lives with me so I figured we would give it a try this evening and it did not disappoint

Me and my niece usually got stay at my sister’s house on the weekends. My sister works Fri,Sat & Sun nights and I got up there to watch her daughter,my 7yr old niece. I’m doing my sister a favor but it also gives my nieces more time to spend together. I look forward to going up there this weekend so we can all 3 play together. Another huge plus about this game is,when playing in VR you remain stationary which makes it possible for my nieces to play as the tree sometimes too. The only games I have let them play were either games where you remain stationary or mixed reality games to prevent motion sickness

In this game you can have as many as 8 friends playing against you on phones/tablets. This game would be a big hit the whole family could enjoy on holidays & family get togethers. It’s also a great way to introduce children to the wonder VR technology or at least include them in your experience. I know this is an old game but it is a hidden gem. I would love to see more developers bring us more party games that work in conjunction with phones/tablets. I love seeing newcomers reactions to VR/MR,especially when it comes to young children,there excitement is unmatched. I will never forget my first VR experiences and I love to share that experience with others

reviews.review_title: So much fun for the whole family!
reviews.review_description: Everyone can jump in and enjoy. Highly recommend!
Fun to play with friends who have phones
This is a great idea and the mobile side of it does not require sign in. Tons of fun and a decent workout.
I love this game me my mum my dad and my brother play it every afternoon
Great party game. Great courses and easy to play. Lots of fun with friends. Be prepared for the noise as the night goes on, it's a riot.
A game full of fun

reviews.review_title: The tutorial is fun but no servers
reviews.review_description: The tutorial is fun but I can't find any servers

This game would be perfect if there was a single player mode where you can beat different challenges like how you do with angry birds. It would be nice to have to complete a board in a certain amount of time to get anywhere from 1 to 3 stars.

I recently downloaded this game for a party, and about half of the people really enjoyed playing.  The half that enjoyed it were people who were regular gamers and could deal with the fixed camera changing positions kind of at random.  The other half just found the fixed camera angles confusing, frustrating, and annoying.  We put it away after about a half hour because so many people were annoyed, and I haven't played since.  If it weren't for the camera, I think it would be a party hit.  As it is, it's a vibe killer.
This has been our family’s long standing favorite game on quest.

This game is an awesome party game! It is super fun with more than one person in the ability to be on your mobile phone playing while the other person and people in the room can be playing as well. Super fun! 
￼

This game requires you to have at least one friend play with you.

That said, this game is amazing! So much fun rotating through who plays the tree and creating strategies. More fun than Jack Box in my opinion!

reviews.review_title: Fun for the tree... Sucks to be a squirr
reviews.review_description: Not fun for the people playing on phones and tablets. Minimum update: Needs second joy stick to rotate camera. Squirrels can't see where to go as the run back. Just frustrating! Also it would be really nice to have controller support. This game is one star without these changes. Pretty much unplayable without these changes
The first game I found allowing up to 9 players to enjoy a great game night simultaneously. Great with friends and family of all ages! Hope there's more game like this out there.
I play with my whole family, and we all take turns being in VR. This game is fun as an adult, but easy enough for my 6 yo to be competitive in either role. We’ve put in several hours in a few days.

This game is a lot of fun if you have friends that are free to play it. I wish they'd make a lobby system for people who are wanting to play with randoms just to have some more game time. Also I'd be nice to have a party chat system in game so if someone is remote everyone can talk and have fun.
Really good fun with friends , I’d recommend it

Just got this yesterday, and been playing with my family. We had a full team of 8 squirrels and 1 tree and it was a blast. My nieces are 4-6 years old and are use to playing on iPads. They love the game! I've played both the tree and a squirrel and found both sides very fun. My only complaint is the camera angle for the squirrels is a bit hard to see when you are returning to the base with a acorn. Some levels of drop offs and a lot of my family can't play those levels because it's frustrating not to see where your going and falling I to a pit. However it is still fun overall. The camera is always focused on the tree, which is nice so you can see when they might throw stuff. But hard to get use to for people who don't play games often. Still amazing game and would highly suggest if you have young kids.

I really love the game at first but after a few games and the players know about the skills and how they work then it quickly becomes way unbalanced. The tree is no match when against 3 or more squirrels. They spawn too quickly and some abilities like digging in the ground is way too overpowered especially when you can connect them from the acorn spawn point all the way to the base. If the devs don't want to balance things then I really hope we are given an option to tweak these things. Like the respawn rates and cooldowns of the skills and tree ammo.

reviews.review_title: The whole family gets to play
reviews.review_description: This game is what every game should strive to have. It seamlessly includes all the family iPads and iPhones so everyone can play as a squirrel and rotate out as the tree! Great fun for everyone.

Now this is a superb game! 
This is a must get if you want to involve other people in your VR experience, Hours of fun!
And when you can't wait for the rest of the family to get home, you can always play on your own just join your phone and have loads of bots it's very amusing. 
I do wish there was more games like this.
A+ devs top game!

This is a fun game, but needs a few fixes.  Several times I respawned under ground and immediately died.  When you are a squirrel, if you stand in front of walls or foliage it blocks your view and you can’t see anything.  Need to make stuff like that transparent if it is that close to the screen.  When I was the tree, there were a few times the tree would not let go of the pinecone when I tried to throw it.  Finally, the AI for the bots is terrible.

developer_response.body: Thank you for letting us know about the issues you've experienced. We'll do our best to look into this. If you want to share any more details on the problems, you're more than welcome to email us at support@resolutiongames.com.

reviews.review_description: Game started out great then the kids figured out a cheat where they tunnel to the acorn and then tunnel away with the acorns. The game became too frustrating for them since there was just split sec where the tree can grab them even though they know what they are doing. I’d change this if I was the devs.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We're taking this to heart for sure. Here are some tips as well for how to handle tunnels (especially if built in a row): Place a boulder at the openings, hit them in between the tunnels by practising on the timing of when the squirrel pops out, place sap prior to squirrels building tunnels (you can't start building a tunnel when you're standing in sap). Hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: Had a blast. Whole fam liked it

reviews.review_title: Great family and game night fun
reviews.review_description: We have started playing this with our kids and at parties with friends its a hit. The controls are simple and for the vr side its a great intro to vr. My child loves being the squirel. The depth of teamwork in the game is really cool as well. Great game for all ages and endless fun.

The game seams fun, but we have never been able to play it for more than 5 minutes before we get constant disconnects.

After trying on many occasions, it always end with frustration because all the mobile clients gets kicked.

developer_response.body: We would love to help you look into this. Please email us at support@resolutiongames.com and we will help you look into it.

reviews.review_title: Fun game for a group with one headset
reviews.review_description: This game was a lot of fun for some friends and myself. We only had one VR headset and we all could play along. Wish there were more games like this

reviews.review_title: So much fun for the family!
reviews.review_description:
This is my first multiplayer game I’ve played that includes my entire family. This was a blast!  My kids loved it. They figured out a game that makes it as enjoyable to be the one outside the VR. 

My wife said “more games like this will get me fully on board with this platform!” - that was an ENORMOUS win for me.

The developer did about 50% of the work for AI-controlled squirrels. They’re  so bad that they actually pick up acorns and return them to the tree. Or they pick up acorns and run around aimlessly. Finish the programming! Update the game! The AI squirrels should be better than human-controlled squirrels!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We did an update to the AIs yesterday but will of course continue to look into how they can become better. We really appreciate your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
This game is very fun for all. It’s a great way to introduce people to oculus. Very fun for all ages and isn’t extremely taxing movement wise. My biggest complaint on this game is that when you are a squirrel on the app and you retrieve an acorn the camera angle doesn’t stay behind you. It makes it impossible to see where you are going on the course map and unfair to the other players. If they fixed 
this it would be a 5 star review

developer_response.body: Thank you for telling us what you think. We know many players would like to see this change in the game and we are keeping this in mind for sure.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic game for the whole family
reviews.review_description:
My kids and I played this game until the battery ran out. We had a blast! Clean family fun, challenging enough to keep the kids engaged, nothing scary and all 3 of my kids could enjoy it (7, 10 and 12). Being the tree teaches them hand eye coordination and focus. Being the squirrels requires planning, team work and quick thinking. I highly recommend this game.

Devs: Consider adding additional mini games and game modes like having one squirrel on the tree’s team that can steal back captured nuts. If another squirrel touches him before he reaches the tree the nut returns to the squirrels.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and the suggestion! Sounds like a fun way to play and we've taken notes on this. :)

reviews.review_description: What a great idea. Everyone loves being the tree, but the different squirrels are so fun to play, no one minds waiting their turn. It really is designed for a group, and great if you only have 1 oculus. With 3 daughters, everyone has a good time. They can’t wait to have friends come over and play with them.  Since it’s a PVP environment, the game is different each time and just makes you want to play 1 more round!
Everyone in my family can play and they all enjoy it! The kids are 4 and 7 years old and they picked up on the controls easily. We look forward to hours of family time together!

I played this with my whole family. The mobile app is free, and you can play with up to 7 squirrels. There are also several different types of squirrels to choose from. Overall we all enjoyed this game as it’s a lot more interactive for the non-VR people in the room.

developer_response.body: You can actually play up to 8 squirrels! Good to know in case you host a big get-together anytime soon. ;)

reviews.review_title: Great game to play with friends
reviews.review_description: Good game you should buy it
